title: practical english usage - Part 236
content:
There are 1000 millimetres in 1 metre.

Spaces are sometimes used instead of commas.

3,164   3164  the year 1946 in dates.

We   do not always use commas in four-figure numbers, and they are never used not 6,435  (     &43S)  7,456,189 separating off the thousands and millions.

Commas are used to divide large numbers into groups of three figures, by 11  numbers ... marketing organisations, banks, and some of the major credit agencies.

organisations, banks and credit agencies.

The developing crisis affected manufacturers, distributors, marketing and unless the last item is long). Compare: We   use commas to separate the different things in a list (but not usually before 10 lists The cowboy was tall, dark and handsome.

His speech was long boring detailed and irrelevant.

After be and other linking verbs, adjectives are punctuated like a list (see below).

surprising new developments gloomy economic forecasts new, financial director) not Have you met our handsome new financial director? (     .. .our handsome.

kinds of information.

Before a noun, commas are not normally used when adjectives give different concrete, glass and plastic buildings not a green, red and gold carpet (     a green-red and gold carpet) parts of something.

Commas are not dropped when adjectives or other modifiers refer to different a tall(,) dark(,) handsome cowboy Commas are sometimes dropped between short adjectives.

an expensive, ill-planned, wasteful project a tall, impressive figure  a long, boring speech or related kinds of information.

Before a noun, we  use commas mostly to separate adjectives that give the same 9 commas      between       adjectives fc  lts not G  A (             began to-dry up, however, some-companies-...) Orders began to dry up; however, some companies tried to carry on as before.

Orders began to dry up. Consequently, most companies started to reduce their semi-colon is necessary between the sentences (►   283).

grammatically separate from the sentence before it; consequently, a full stop or adverbs, not conjunctions. A  sentence beginning with an adverb is Note that some linking words (e.g. consequently, however, therefore) are 8  conjunctions         and   adverbs Written Texts   Section 26 grammar •  297  punctuation: dash American usage often prefers double quotation marks in these cases.

A textbook can be a 'wall' between the teacher and the class.

People disagree about how to use the word 'disinterested'.

special meanings.

special ways - for example when we  talk about them or when we  give them We   often put quotation marks (usually single) round words which  are used in 2 special     use   of  words in these cases.

American usage often prefers single quotation marks inside double 'His last words,' said Albert, 'were "Close that window".' (or single inside double).

For quotations inside quotations, we  use double quotation marks inside single We   use quotation marks (single or double) when we  quote direct speech.

1  direct     speech ‘ inverted commas’  in British English.

Quotation marks can be single ('...') or double (''...”  ). They  are also called 298 punctuatio       n: quota    tion    marks    _____ For the use of hyphens (as in hard-working or co-operative), ►    342.

And then we met Joseph - with Lisa, believe it or not!

We'll be arriving on Monday morning - at least, I think so.

A  dash can introduce an afterthought, or something unexpected and surprising.

My mother - who rarely gets angry - really lost her temper.

We had a great time in Greece - the kids really loved it.

the other.

There are three things I can never remember - names, faces, and I've forgotten same way as colons, semi-colons or brackets.

Dashes (-) are especially common in informal writing.
